summary: Session 3: module authoring, scaffolding & ownership templates. New-module scaffold (routed ruling Q1, saved raw at s3-authoring-scaffold-architecture.json; anthropic excluded after a provider-side 400 x3 - S2 precedent - so gemini-pro ruled, auto-verified VERIFIED): shared unit-testable logic in src/utils/moduleAuthoring.ts (kebab slug validation against the manifest, format-preserving append guarded by a parse-after-append refusal that writes NOTHING and prints the copyable entry block when the appended text cannot land in the modules: list, header-seeded docs/modules.yaml creation, explicit planPath, skip-existing plan stub written stub-first), flow in src/commands/newModule.ts (two InputBoxes with live validateInput; stub opened after creation), exposed BOTH as the dabbler.newModule palette command and as the riderless new-module Getting Started action (protocol union + dispatch case + step-2 secondary button with explainer hover copy). Module-targeted authoring (rulings Q2/Q4): BootstrapContext.module + the whole-line {{MODULE_LINE}} token in spec.md.template (VERIFICATION_MODE_LINE pattern) so the decomposition prompt's worked exemplar STAMPS module: <slug> writer-rendered; copySessionSetGenPrompt resolves the target through the shared pickModuleForAuthoring (no manifest = unchanged pre-087 flow; one module = auto-select WITH an operator-visible notice; >=2 = QuickPick; Esc cancels the whole copy) and references the module's planPath (existence-checked, module-aware missing-plan warning); a hard-requirements line demands the stamp and guidance RECOMMENDS (never enforces) the module slug in set names; copyPlanningPrompt + importPlanFromFile share the same picker and target the module plan, with repo-level renders byte-identical pre-087 and the no-root error deliberately kept AFTER the file dialog to preserve the Set 060 cancelled-dialog semantics. Ownership + CI templates (ruling Q3): CODEOWNERS.template (comment-only, worked Priya/Sam/Alex example, integration `touches` review rule taught as process - CODEOWNERS semantics are path-based) and monorepo-ci.yml.template (commented dorny/paths-filter per-module jobs + the single ACTIVE all-module guardrail job on push to main whose placeholder step succeeds, so the unadapted file can never break a build) loaded via TemplateBundle and written by BOTH scaffold paths to .github/CODEOWNERS + .github/workflows/monorepo-ci.yml (skip-existing); the esbuild required-file gate gains both PLUS the previously-missing cross-provider-verification.md.template (a Set 077 sibling-site gap, L-069-1); the bundle README inventory updated (including the missing Set 077 row). Verification round 1 (gpt-5-6) found one Major (Correctness): pickModuleForAuthoring treated a PRESENT-but-invalid docs/modules.yaml like an absent one, silently falling back to unstamped repo-level authoring; fixed in-flight - classifyModulesManifest distinguishes absent/invalid/present (lstat entry check), an invalid manifest now errors with the shared INVALID_MANIFEST_MESSAGE and ABORTS every authoring flow (new invalid-manifest outcome handled by copyPlanningPrompt, importPlanFromFile before its dialog, and copySessionSetGenPrompt; scaffoldNewModule reuses the classifier), pinned by 4 new tests. Evidence (final): unit suite 1350 passing / 0 failing (37 net-new tests incl. the moduleAuthoring matrix with every refusal case), tsc + esbuild clean, eslint at the 7-error pre-existing baseline (new files fully clean), Layer 1 pytest 2922 passed / 6 skipped / 0 failed (the cold-start acceptance test consumed the regenerated goldens, which now carry the two .github teaching templates per tier), and a live 12/12-PASS dogfood that drove the real flow/picker/prompt-builders end-to-end against a scratch repo. Operator answered the requiresUAT/requiresE2E 'suggested' prompt with 'uat' (suggestion_disposition, session 3): the armed gate is the ad-hoc human checklist - authored to the Set-078 bar as 087-module-organized-projects-foundations-uat-checklist.json (8 walks; every functional item pre-verified by a named suite and/or the live dogfood; runs against the locally built, untracked 0.41.0 VSIX; awaiting the operator's walk - pending UAT blocks downstream sessions, not this close). Code commit 66ed06d pushed; CI run 29131125452 (Playwright Layer 3 on all three OSes, required by L-064-12 for the webview form change even though the armed operator gate is UAT) is the Layer-3 evidence of record - final status recorded in the activity log.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (s3-next-orchestrator-analysis.json, gemini-pro) recommends an Opus-class Claude seat at HIGH effort for Session 4: the deliverables are long-form, coherence-critical prose (the three-person Hello World walkthrough + the AI feedback review prompt) with strict stylistic constraints, plus the set-terminal ceremony (change-log, Step 9 review, advisory path-aware critique, release prep). Supervision points (routed): the operator executes the walkthrough verbatim in a clean environment; dogfooded review-prompt output is judged on coaching tone and cited-evidence quality, not just on flagging the planted violation; a generic path-aware critique is rejected - it must ground in Set 087's actual events.

deferred.item: Explorer UI affordance for duplicateNameError (row badge/tooltip + at most one throttled notification)
deferred.reason: Carried from the S2 disposition unchanged: anticipated by the S1 collision ruling but absent from the operator-approved spec's S2-S4 plans; S3's spec scope (authoring/scaffolding/templates) did not include it and the scope-doubt rule forbids unilateral expansion. The fail-loud data-layer behavior (flagged winner row + deduped console.error) shipped in S1 and is live. Candidate for the 088 follow-on set.

deferred.item: Harmonize the LEGACY single-implicit Explorer dialect onto the conformant ARIA tree pattern (S2 deferral)
deferred.reason: Carried from the S2 disposition unchanged: the single-implicit dialect keeps pre-087 byte-identical markup by operator-approved spec constraint; relaxing it needs its own operator sign-off. Candidate for 088 or a dedicated a11y pass.

deferred.item: The Getting Started form's step-2 'planPresent' completion flag keys only on the repo-level docs/planning/project-plan.md; a module plan import/creation does not flip the step complete
deferred.reason: The flag is Set 060 D3 scope and the form is a fresh-repo surface; redefining its semantics (which plan counts as 'the plan' in a module-organized repo) was not in the S3 plan and touches the D3 watcher contract. Surfaced per the scope-doubt rule; candidate for 088 or a small follow-on.

deferred.item: Human UAT walk of the S3 checklist (8 walks, local 0.41.0 VSIX)
deferred.reason: The operator-armed ad-hoc gate: the checklist is authored, mechanically pre-verified (every functional item carries ProgrammaticVerification), and pending the operator's walk. Per Rule 9 pending UAT blocks downstream sessions on this surface until reviewed; the operator owns pacing. S4 (tutorials + set close) should confirm the walk's outcome before the set-terminal release prep.
